title: Arkansas FOIA Request – Sustained Misconduct & Brady Referral Records (Jan 1 2000 – PRESENT) (Office Of Mayor City Of Wynne)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arkansas Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Re: Arkansas FOIA Request – Sustained Misconduct & Brady Referral Records (Jan 1 2000 – PRESENT)

Dear Custodian:

Under the Arkansas Freedom of Information Act, Ark. Code Ann. § 25-19-101 et seq., please provide electronic copies of the following records created or maintained between January 1 2000 to Present:

A. Internal Affairs & Discipline  
1. Investigative reports, case files, findings, and final disposition letters for all **sustained** internal affairs cases involving:  
   a. Untruthfulness, perjury, falsification of documents, or other credibility violations;  
   b. Evidence mishandling or tampering;  
   c. Excessive force or civil-rights violations;  
   d. Bias-based policing or discrimination;  
   e. Criminal acts or domestic violence by officers.

B. Brady / Giglio Referrals  
2. Any letters, memoranda, spreadsheets, or electronic transmissions sent to any Prosecuting Attorney’s Office placing officers on a Brady, Giglio, impeachment, or “no-call” list.  
3. All current and historical lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other compilations maintained by this agency identifying officers referred for Brady/Giglio purposes.

C. Policies & Procedures  
4. Policies, general orders, SOPs, training bulletins, or directives that explain how the department identifies, documents, or communicates potential impeachment material to prosecutors.

D. Certification / Decertification  
5. Records reflecting any officer referrals to, or actions by, the Arkansas Commission on Law Enforcement Standards and Training (CLEST) for decertification, suspension, or revocation based on misconduct or credibility issues.

Please produce the records electronically when possible. I request a waiver of any fees because disclosure is in the public interest. If costs are anticipated, please notify me before proceeding.

If you claim any exemption, please cite the specific statutory provision and provide all segregable portions per Ark. Code Ann. § 25-19-105(f).

I look forward to your response within three (3) business days, as required by AFOIA. Thank you for your cooperation.

Respectfully,

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Christopher Reed

title: 1st Judicial Circuit of Arkansas Courts Public Records Request – Brady / Giglio Material

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arkansas Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Re: Public Records Request – Brady / Giglio Material

Dear Records Officer:

Pursuant to [cite state open-records law, e.g., “the Colorado Open Records Act, C.R.S. § 24-72-201 et seq.”], I respectfully request access to and copies of the following records for the period [start date] through [end date]:

1. Any and all “Brady” or “Giglio” lists, spreadsheets, databases, memoranda, or other compilations that identify law-enforcement officers whose conduct has been flagged for disclosure under Brady v. Maryland, 373 U.S. 83 (1963), and Giglio v. United States, 405 U.S. 150 (1972).  
2. Any policies, standard operating procedures, manuals, memoranda, training materials, or other documents that describe how this Office identifies, reviews, updates, and discloses potential impeachment material relating to law-enforcement witnesses (a/k/a “Brady policy” or “Giglo protocol”).  
3. Any correspondence, referral letters, or electronic communications between this Office and any law-enforcement agency concerning the placement, retention, or removal of officers on or from a Brady/Giglio disclosure list.  
4. Logs or indices reflecting the dates on which individual officers were placed on, or removed from, any Brady/Giglio list.  
5. Any periodic or annual reports, audits, or statistical summaries concerning Brady/Giglio disclosures prepared by or for this Office.  

I request that all fees be waived because the requested records are sought in the public interest and will contribute significantly to public understanding of government operations. If fees cannot be waived, please inform me of the anticipated costs before processing.

If any part of this request is denied, please cite each specific statutory exemption relied upon and release all segregable portions. I am willing to accept records in electronic format.

Please confirm receipt of this request and contact me if needed

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Christopher Reed

title: CMS.gov: Regulatory, Policy or Legal Authority Regarding Decision to Deploy llms.txt file at https://design.cms.gov/llms.txt

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: CMS.gov: Regulatory, Policy or Legal Authority Regarding Decision to Deploy llms.txt file at https://design.cms.gov/llms.txt
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records relating to the regulatory, policy, or legal authority that governed or informed the decision to implement an llms.txt file at design.cms.gov, including:

Any written policy, directive, memorandum, or guidance from the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) or CMS authorizing or requiring the deployment of llms.txt files on agency websites;
Any legal review or Section 508 compliance analysis conducted in connection with the deployment of the llms.txt file at design.cms.gov;
Any records referencing OMB guidance, Executive Orders, or federal IT policy directives (including those issued under 44 U.S.C. § 3501 et seq.) that were cited as authority for this deployment;
Any records reflecting whether HHS issued a department-wide policy on AI crawler file standards prior to, or concurrent with, this implementation.
Time scope: January 1, 2024 through the date this request is processed.

This: https://design.cms.gov/llms.txt is one of the first llms.txt files deployed from a federal .gov domain. 

Format: Electronic records in native format preferred.

I request a fee waiver under 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(iii) on the basis that disclosure is in the public interest, as this request concerns the use of public resources to deploy emerging AI-related web standards on a federal government website, and is not for commercial purposes.

If any responsive records are withheld in full or in part, please provide a Vaughn index identifying each withheld record and the exemption claimed. I look forward to your acknowledgment within 20 business days as required by statute. That you to CMS.gov's commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware
